mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and 20-day forward returns is negative or insignificantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 0.25% marks the exit from the zero lower bound and initiates a discount-rate tightening channel that reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or the conditional mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated CPIAUCSL inflation and a suppressed FEDFUNDS rate create deeply negative real policy rates that crowd capital out of nominal fixed income and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is non-additive because either condition alone does not trigger the same reallocation intensity.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or an interaction term adds no incremental R-squared (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5.0%, additional month-over-month declines in UNRATE signal an overheating labor market that raises the probability of imminent monetary tightening, which in turn produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0% and decreases month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances or the conditional mean is not negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and operating leverage that translates directly into earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly INDPRO change and 5-day forward returns is negative or insignificantly positive (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer spending momentum that drags down top-line revenue expectations for consumer-facing corporations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are not negative on average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) creates a self-reinforcing income-output loop that confirms broad-based expansion, driving earnings revisions higher than either indicator alone and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: An interaction term between monthly changes in PAYEMS and INDPRO does not significantly improve prediction of 60-day returns beyond additive terms (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and construction employment that foreshadows broader demand deterioration, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,500, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative on average, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.
